world.title: Impact Community Projects (Kosovo)
world.description: Deadline: 06-May-2026 The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI) 2026 in Kosovo provides funding between CAD 30,000 and CAD 100,000 for small-scale, high-impact development projects led by local organizations. It prioritizes inclusive governance, peace and security, and inclusive economic growth. Projects must be community-driven, results-focused, and completed by February 2027 or February 2028 depending on

world.title: Climate disasters wreck schools, derail millions of learners
world.description: Climate disasters are devastating schools across Eastern and Southern Africa, disrupting millions of learners and costing billions in damages and lost future earnings.

world.title: King's visit to 'revitalise' relationship with US
world.description: The UK's ambassador to the US welcomes the state visit, at a difficult time for UK and US relations.

world.title: Iran offers "new proposal" via Pakistan to end war, reopen Strait of Hormuz: Report
world.description: Tehran has proposed a "two-stage plan" via Pakistan to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. While Iran seeks to delay nuclear talks until the US blockade is lifted, President Trump remains sceptical, urging direct contact while insisting Iran must never acquire nuclear weapons.

world.title: Israel sent its Iron Dome and troops to UAE for fighting Iranian missiles: Report
world.description: Israel sent “an Iron Dome air defense system with troops to operate it,” the report said, citing two Israeli officials and one US official. | World News

world.title: Middle East war live: Putin receives Iran’s foreign minister amid stalled US-Iran talks
world.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin plans to meet with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Monday, Russian state media reported. Russia and Tehran, who are both subject to fierce Western sanctions,…

world.description: Pakistani mediators have given the proposal to the White House, but it's unclear whether the US wants to explore it, an Axios report said

world.title: Negeri Sembilan Chief Minister loses majority
world.description: Negeri Sembilan's Chief Minister has lost his majority after UMNO assemblymen withdrew support amid a political crisis involving state ruler and chieftains. Read more at straitstimes.com.

business.title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures
business.description: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

business.title: WA's far north 'blindsided' by Boskalis's marine sand proposal and lack of consultation
business.description: A Dutch dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

business.title: Chery Arrizo S debuts in Beijing: Audi-look sedan packs 192 kW turbo muscle
business.description: Chery reveals Arrizo S sedan with 192 kW 2.0T engine, expanding lineup toward higher-performance B-class segment at Beijing Auto Show.

business.title: Stocks Rise on Iran Offer Report, Oil Pares Gain: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Stocks advanced and crude oil pared gains following a report Iran offered the US a proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lifting sentiment as efforts to restart peace talks had stalled.

business.title: Iran peace talks stall
business.description: Oil prices rose on Monday morning in Asia after plans for a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran stalled again.

business.title: Trade setup for April 27: Can NIFTY50 hold the opening gains on Monday? Check details
business.description: The GIFT NIFTY futures indicate a strong gap-up opening on Monday morning, owing to positive global market cues. The Asian markets rejoiced in the rally in the US tech stocks. The open interest data for tomorrow's expiry indicates strong resistance at the 24,200 levels.

business.title: BSNL ARPU Jumps 42% to Rs 101 in FY26 from Rs 71
business.description: BSNL reports a sharp 42% ARPU growth in FY26, rising from Rs 71 to Rs 101.

A mysterious “golden orb” discovered deep in the ocean in 2023 puzzled scientists for years. Found more than 3,000 meters below

science.title: NASA's Dragonfly Comes Together Amid Harsh Testing
science.description: NASA's Dragonfly explorer is coming together as testing is underway. The spacecraft is slated to explore Saturn's moon Titan.

science.title: Master hunters: Neanderthals were able to hunt elephants; proof is out
science.description: The new paper proposes that original researchers operated on the flawed assumption that any sign of butchery had been erased.

science.title: Scientists Find Peculiar Differences in Two Uranian Rings
science.description: The planet Uranus is a weird place. Not only does it roll around the Sun on its side once every 84.3 Earth years, it also sports a spindly set of rings corralled in some places by strange little moons. Two of those rings, the μ (mu) and ν (nu) rings are incredibly faint, which makes them challenging to study.

science.title: The world’s most powerful telescope was switched on in Chile and may finally solve the mystery of the ninth planet that scientists believe has been hidden in the Solar System for billions of years.
science.description: The Vera Rubin Telescope, installed in Chile with the largest digital camera ever built, will scan the Southern Hemisphere sky from 2025 and could locate the hypothetical ninth planet of the Solar System in the next two years, a celestial body ten times the mass of Earth proposed by Caltech astronomers to explain anomalous orbits of […]

science.title: NASA confirms, two airplane-sized asteroids to make close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026
science.description: Two airplane-size asteroids, 2026 HJ3 and 2026 HR, are set to make a safe close approach to Earth on 27 April 2026, passing millions of miles away with no risk of impact.

science.title: Scientists Need Your Help to Find Galaxies Bending Light in Space
science.description: Imagine holding a wine glass up to a candle (of course I had to pour a glass to try this.) The curved glass bends and distorts the flame, stretching it into arcs and rings of light.

science.title: Scientists Were Wrong About This Strange “Rule-Breaking” Particle
science.description: A long-suspected crack in particle physics may have closed, but the search for what lies beyond continues.

ai.title: ET Awards: ‘Invest in the idea of India, by India, for India,’ say top CEOs amid West Asia conflict
ai.description: Indian business leaders view the West Asia conflict as a catalyst for long-delayed structural reforms, urging accelerated efforts in energy security and reduced external vulnerabilities. They advocate for greater self-reliance in energy sources and a significant push towards electric vehicles, while also emphasizing the need for workforce upskilling to navigate the disruptions brought by Artificial Intelligence.

ai.title: Oil Price Surge Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions Sparks Inflation Worries
ai.description: Oil prices soared due to ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict impacting Middle East energy exports. Stalled peace talks increased market instability, notably affecting Brent crude futures and chip stocks. Markets remained watchful of central bank meetings while AI-driven optimism temporarily alleviated investor concerns over potential inflationary pressures.

ai.title: Chips carry stocks higher; Oil jumps on stalled peace talks
ai.description: Oil climbed on Monday as stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks prolonged the disruption of Middle East energy exports, while renewed excitement about artificial intelligence spending drove up chip stocks at...

ai.title: Stage set for Elon Musk's court battle with OpenAI
ai.description: As the courtroom doors swing open on Monday, tech titan Elon Musk steps into a fierce clash against AI powerhouse OpenAI. Musk argues that the organization has strayed from its foundational mission of serving the greater good. With OpenAI's ChatGPT challenging Musk's own AI initiative, Grok, the stakes couldn't be higher.

ai.title: Google’s big India bet: $15 Billion Vizag AI data centre foundation to be laid on April 28
ai.description: The project spans 600 acres across three locations: Rambilli, Adavivaram, and Tarluvada, which is projected to deploy a subsea gateway and provide gigawatt-scale compute for global operations.

ai.title: China expands economic pressure tools in US rivalry ahead of Trump-Xi summit
ai.description: China is building new economic pressure tools against the United States. Beijing has enacted laws to punish foreign firms shifting supply chains. It has also tightened rare earth licensing and banned foreign AI chips. These actions signal a strategic move to counter U.S. measures. China aims to defend its interests and expand its influence.

ai.title: TPIsoftware Wins "Taiwan SMART 50 Award" for Its FHIR-Based Medical AI Application
ai.description: **media[1030052]**TAIPEI, April 26, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- TPIsoftware (TPEx: 7781) was honored with the first Taiwan SMART 50 Award for creating a FHIR-based Medical AI Application. Organized by the Ministry of Health and Welfare, the award encourages the development of smart health apps that align with the SMART on FHIR standard to enable secure and real-time data sharing across different hospitals. 50 apps were selected from 115 submissions through an open call. These apps will be listed on the Taiwan Health App Space (THAS) as part of the country's initiative to foster data interoperability in healthcare.

ai.title: Elon Musk Is an Underdog in His $180 Billion Fight Against OpenAI
ai.description: The lawsuit seeks to oust Sam Altman and claw back billions, testing legal theories about nonprofit governance. | World News
